title: Instructional Resources: Library Materials
description: Conroe ISD's guidelines for library materials explain how books and other resources are selected, maintained, and made available in school libraries. The policy also establishes a process for community members and parents to raise concerns about specific materials they believe may be inappropriate for students. Understanding these guidelines helps families know how library collections are managed and how they can participate if they have questions or objections about available materials.

timeline_local.summary: Conroe ISD's Policy EFB (Local) on Instructional Resources: Library Materials was comprehensively rewritten, removing the prior framework (collection development standards, selection criteria, parental involvement procedures, and the informal/formal reconsideration committee process) and replacing it with a new structure that emphasizes Board and Superintendent oversight, a formal prohibition on pornographic material, Superintendent certification of library materials for compliance, Board approval of all library material acquisitions, and a streamlined parent opt-out and challenge process aligned with Texas Education Code §§ 33.021–33.027.

description_local: This policy governs the selection, use, and removal of library and instructional materials in the district's schools, including new rules about what types of content are prohibited. It applies to all library books and teaching materials available to students, and establishes clear definitions around sexually explicit or pornographic content that cannot be included in school collections. Parents and community members should be aware of this policy because it directly shapes what reading and learning materials students can access at school, and it reflects recent state legislation aimed at setting stricter content standards for school libraries.
